intro: Sciodaphyllum breviramum, also known as the West Indian arrowroot or Mayan arrowroot, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of wounds, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Bioactive constituents of Sciodaphyllum breviramum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
